sources.notes: Sum powers of omega(a_i a_j) over unordered pairs, where omega is the number of distinct prime factors.
Official editorial uses omega(xy)=omega(x)+omega(y)-omega(gcd(x,y)) and counts pairs by gcd with multiples DP and subtraction over multiples.

annotation.summary: For an array, compute a sum over pairs involving the k-th power of the number of distinct prime factors of the product.
annotation.constraints: Values are bounded, and omega(x) is at most 6 under the constraints, so states over omega sums are tiny while gcd values range over all divisors.
annotation.core_idea: Use omega(xy)=omega(x)+omega(y)-omega(gcd(x,y)). Count ordered pairs grouped by exact gcd g and by omega(x)+omega(y). First count numbers divisible by each g and each omega value, then perform a multiples Möbius-style subtraction from large g down to obtain exact-gcd pair counts. Sum (sumLen-omega(g))^k over those counts.
annotation.complexity: O(maxA log maxA * K^2) with small K <= 6.

solution_variants.name: gcd-multiple-dp
solution_variants.summary: Count pairs by exact gcd and omega-sum using divisor multiples and subtract counts of larger gcd multiples.

solution_variants.name: gcd-convolution
solution_variants.summary: Alternative solution groups values by omega and applies GCD convolution/zeta-Möbius transforms.

tags.evidence: Exact gcd counts are obtained by subtracting contributions of larger multiples, the standard divisor Möbius/inclusion-exclusion pattern.
tags.description: Möbius inversion or equivalent subtract-over-multiples inclusion-exclusion on divisors.
tags.created_reason: Möbius/divisor inversion is a common advanced number-theory technique worth searching directly.

tags.evidence: The editorial comments also describe a GCD convolution alternative over omega classes.
tags.description: Convolution over gcd or divisor lattice, usually implemented with zeta/Möbius transforms on multiples.
tags.created_reason: GCD convolution is a specific transform technique useful for locating advanced number-theory problems.

tags.evidence: The core identity and all states depend on omega(x), the number of distinct prime factors.
tags.description: Use counts of distinct or total prime factors as states, weights, or invariants.
tags.created_reason: Prime-factor-count problems are more specific than general number theory.

tags.evidence: The solution counts valid unordered/ordered pairs across divisor and omega classes.
